principles.name: Verifiable proof beats pattern-matching
principles.principle: Gates accept typed, verifiable evidence, never free-text that happens to contain the right substring.
principles.why: A substring gate is trivially satisfiable by writing the required string into any field, so it passes work that was never done.
principles.open_work: SL-3 (typed ProofArtifact evidence replacing the substring gate)

principles.name: Measure your own gates (false-pass rate)
principles.principle: A gate is only trusted once its false-pass rate is measured against injected faults and the baseline is committed.
principles.why: An unmeasured gate can wave through broken work indefinitely because nobody knows how often it lets failures pass.

principles.name: Prove invariants in CI, don't assert them
principles.principle: Critical invariants are checked by an executable test in CI, not asserted in prose or a comment.
principles.why: An invariant that is only asserted drifts silently the first time someone changes one of its two sides without anyone noticing.

principles.name: Coordinate through canonical state, not status files
principles.principle: Agents coordinate by reading and writing canonical Events, not by parsing each other's free-form status files.
principles.why: Status files have no schema and no ordering guarantees, so coordination built on them races and silently disagrees.
principles.open_work: SL-4 (promote status-file coordination to canonical Events)

principles.name: Score spec assumptions, not just tasks
principles.principle: Risk scoring ranks the spec's assumptions by blast radius and uncertainty, not just its enumerated tasks.
principles.why: Scoring only tasks leaves the most dangerous wrong assumptions unranked, so the plan optimizes effort while ignoring the real risk.
principles.open_work: SL-6 (score assumptions by blast_radius x uncertainty)

principles.name: Close the loop on failure, not just success
principles.principle: A failed wave is recorded as a first-class learnable event, the same way a successful one is.
principles.why: If only successes are captured, every failure is forgotten and the system relearns the same mistake on the next run.
principles.open_work: make a failed wave a first-class learnable event (substrate: events.jsonl)

principles.name: Conflicts live at the contract level, not the file level
principles.principle: Conflicts between agents are detected against declared output contracts and a post-apply drift check, not against raw file overlap.
principles.why: File-level conflict detection both misses semantic clashes in non-overlapping files and flags harmless coincidental edits as conflicts.

principles.name: Sequence by credibility risk, not demonstrability
principles.principle: Work is sequenced so the most load-bearing yet least-proven claims are addressed first, not the easiest ones to demo.
principles.why: Sequencing by demonstrability ships the impressive parts while the claims that would sink the project's credibility stay unproven.

embodied_in.mechanism: integrity-first track orders SL-1..SL-6 by credibility risk ahead of demonstrability

principles.open_work: encode the ordering as an automated check

principles.name: Advisory and enforcing share one code path
principles.principle: The advisory preview and the enforcing transition call the exact same gate predicate, never two parallel implementations.
principles.why: Two implementations of the same gate drift apart, so the preview a reviewer trusts stops matching the check that actually enforces.

embodied_in.mechanism: transitions._evidence_complete delegates to review.gates.evidence_complete (single source of truth)

principles.open_work: extend the agreement-test pattern to fakoli-flow's preview/enforce paths
